questions.question: What is the purpose of the sizeof operator in C?
options: A) To check the data type of a variable
B) To get the size of an array
C) To allocate memory for a new variable
D) To deallocate memory
questions.explanation: The sizeof operator is used to get the size of a data type or an array.

questions.question: What is the difference between a 1D array and a 2D array?
options: A) A 2D array can hold multiple arrays
B) A 2D array is a subset of a 1D array
C) A 2D array has more elements than a 1D array
D) A 1D array can hold multiple variables
questions.explanation: A 2D array is an array of arrays, whereas a 1D array is an array of single elements.

questions.question: How do you initialize a 2D array in C?
options: A) Using the assignment operator (=)
B) Using the sizeof operator
C) By declaring an array of arrays
D) By using a loop
questions.explanation: You initialize a 2D array by declaring an array of arrays.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the arrays.h header file in C?
options: A) To define the size of an array
B) To initialize arrays
C) To declare arrays
D) To use dynamic memory allocation
questions.explanation: The arrays.h header file is used to declare arrays and other related functions.

questions.question: What happens if you assign an array to another array of the same size in C?
options: A) Both arrays are copied
B) The array on the left is assigned the values of the array on the right
C) Both arrays are lost
D) An error occurs
questions.explanation: If you assign an array to another array of the same size, both arrays get the same values.
